content.problemStatement:
There is a contest containing 222 problems AAA and BBB.

222 strong participants PPP and QQQ participated in the contest and solved both the problems.

PPP made AC submissions on problems AAA and BBB at time instants PAP_APA​ and PBP_BPB​ respectively while QQQ made AC submissions on problems AAA and BBB at time instants QAQ_AQA​ and QBQ_BQB​.

It is given that the time penalty is the minimum time instant at which a participant has solved both the problems. Also the participant with the lower time penalty will have a better rank.

Determine which participant got the better rank or if there is a `TIE`.


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, four integers PA,PB,QA,QBP_A, P_B, Q_A, Q_BPA​,PB​,QA​,QB​.


Output Format
For each test case, output `P` if PPP got a better rank, `Q` if QQQ got a better rank, `TIE` otherwise.


Note that output is case-insensitive i.e. `P` and `p` both are considered the same.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111:

Time penalty incurred by participant P=10P = 10P=10.
Time penalty incurred by participant Q=12Q = 12Q=12.

Since 10<1210 \lt 1210<12, PPP gets a better rank.
Test Case 222:

Time penalty incurred by participant P=30P = 30P=30.
Time penalty incurred by participant Q=15Q = 15Q=15.

Since 15<3015 \lt 3015<30, QQQ gets a better rank.
Test Case 333:

Time penalty incurred by participant P=20P = 20P=20.
Time penalty incurred by participant Q=20Q = 20Q=20.

Since 20=2020 = 2020=20, PPP and QQQ gets a same rank (TIE).
Test Case 444:

Time penalty incurred by participant P=6P = 6P=6.
Time penalty incurred by participant Q=6Q = 6Q=6.

Since 6=66 = 66=6, PPP and QQQ gets a same rank (TIE).
